checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for tb-001: Is the sidecar subnet validation correctly enforced in the gossip validation pipeline?
checklist.test_procedure:
1. Identify the gossip validation handler for data column sidecars in the P2P layer.
2. Locate the implementation of compute_subnet_for_data_column_sidecar(sidecar.index) and verify it is called during validation.
3. Craft a test sidecar with index=5 and attempt to publish it on subnet_id=0 (incorrect subnet).
4. Verify that the sidecar is REJECTED with an appropriate error indicating subnet mismatch.
5. Repeat with various index/subnet combinations to ensure all mismatches are rejected.
6. Verify that a correctly matched sidecar (index=5 on subnet_id=5) is accepted.
7. Check that no cross-subnet pollution occurs by monitoring subnet message queues.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-29, Trust Boundary: tb-001, Threat: stride-024

checklist.title: Does the P2P peer correctly reject sidecars with mismatched subnet/index before forwarding to the beacon node?
checklist.test_procedure:
1. Trace the message flow from P2P peer reception to beacon node processing.
2. Verify that subnet validation occurs at the P2P layer BEFORE any message is passed to the beacon node.
3. Inject a malformed sidecar (index=3 on subnet_id=7) at the P2P peer layer.
4. Confirm the message is dropped at the P2P layer and never reaches the beacon node.
5. Check logs/metrics to verify rejection is recorded with the correct reason code.
6. Verify no partial processing or state changes occur in the beacon node for rejected sidecars.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-29, Node: actor-p2p-peer, Trust Boundary: tb-001

checklist.title: Does the beacon node have defense-in-depth validation for sidecar subnet matching as a secondary check?
checklist.test_procedure:
1. Review the beacon node's sidecar processing code for any secondary subnet validation.
2. If secondary validation exists, verify it uses the same compute_subnet_for_data_column_sidecar function.
3. Simulate a scenario where P2P validation is bypassed (e.g., direct internal call) with a mismatched sidecar.
4. Verify the beacon node rejects or handles the invalid sidecar gracefully.
5. If no secondary validation exists, document this as a defense-in-depth gap.
6. Verify that accepted sidecars are stored/processed with correct subnet associations.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-29, Node: actor-beacon-node, Supporting check for defense-in-depth

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for tb-001: Does the gossip validation pipeline correctly IGNORE (not REJECT) sidecars with future slots?
checklist.test_procedure:
1. Identify the gossip validation handler for blob sidecars in the P2P layer (primary element: p2p:SG-006:q_check_future_slot).
2. Review the validation logic that checks block_header.slot against current_slot.
3. Verify that sidecars with block_header.slot > current_slot return IGNORE status, not REJECT.
4. Confirm that IGNORE responses do not trigger peer penalties or scoring decrements.
5. Test by sending sidecars with slots 1, 10, 100, and MAX_UINT64 slots in the future and verify consistent IGNORE behavior.
6. Check that IGNORED sidecars are not permanently discarded but can be re-validated after clock advancement.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-30, Trust Boundary: tb-001, Primary Element: p2p:SG-006:q_check_future_slot. Attacker can flood node with far-future-slot sidecars causing resource exhaustion of the IGNORE queue.

checklist.title: Is the P2P peer handling of future-slot sidecars resilient to resource exhaustion attacks?
checklist.test_procedure:
1. Locate the queue or buffer that stores IGNORED future-slot sidecars in the actor-p2p-peer component.
2. Determine if there is a bounded size limit on this queue.
3. Send a flood of far-future-slot sidecars from a malicious peer and monitor memory consumption.
4. Verify that the queue has appropriate eviction policies (e.g., LRU, time-based expiry).
5. Check that excessive IGNORE entries do not cause memory exhaustion or degrade node performance.
6. Confirm that legitimate future-slot sidecars (e.g., next slot) are not evicted by attacker-controlled far-future entries.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-30, Node: actor-p2p-peer. PoC vector: flood node with far-future-slot sidecars causing resource exhaustion.

checklist.title: Does the beacon node correctly re-validate previously IGNORED future-slot sidecars after clock advancement?
checklist.test_procedure:
1. Locate the re-validation mechanism in the actor-beacon-node component that processes previously IGNORED sidecars.
2. Verify that when current_slot advances, sidecars with block_header.slot <= new_current_slot are re-evaluated.
3. Test by sending a sidecar for slot N+1 when current slot is N, then advance the clock to slot N+1.
4. Confirm the previously IGNORED sidecar is now accepted and processed correctly.
5. Check for race conditions between clock advancement and sidecar re-validation.
6. Verify that sidecars are not validated multiple times causing duplicate processing.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-30, Node: actor-beacon-node. Future-slot sidecars must become valid after clock advancement without loss or duplication.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for tb-001: Does the gossip validation pipeline enforce sidecar.slot > parent.slot with strict inequality?
checklist.test_procedure:
1. Locate the gossip validation code handling sidecar slot validation (p2p:SG-006:q_check_slot_higher).
2. Review the comparison operator used: verify it is strictly greater than (>) not greater than or equal (>=).
3. Craft a test sidecar message where sidecar.slot == parent.slot exactly.
4. Submit the crafted sidecar via P2P gossip protocol.
5. Verify the sidecar is REJECTED (not accepted or marked as valid).
6. Test edge case where sidecar.slot == parent.slot + 1 to confirm valid sidecars are accepted.
7. Test edge case where sidecar.slot < parent.slot to confirm rejection.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-31, Trust Boundary: tb-001, Primary Element: p2p:SG-006:q_check_slot_higher

checklist.title: Does actor-p2p-peer correctly propagate or reject sidecars based on slot ordering validation?
checklist.test_procedure:
1. Identify the P2P peer's message handling code for incoming sidecars.
2. Trace the validation flow from message receipt to acceptance/rejection decision.
3. Verify the peer does not propagate sidecars that fail slot ordering validation.
4. Check that peer scoring/reputation mechanisms penalize peers sending invalid sidecars.
5. Confirm no bypass exists that allows unvalidated sidecar propagation.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-31, Node: actor-p2p-peer, Supporting check for boundary validation

checklist.title: Does actor-beacon-node maintain chain ordering invariant by rejecting sidecars with slot <= parent.slot?
checklist.test_procedure:
1. Identify the beacon node's sidecar processing logic after gossip validation.
2. Verify the beacon node has defense-in-depth validation for slot ordering.
3. Check that accepted sidecars do not corrupt the chain state or fork choice.
4. Test that sidecars with slot == parent.slot do not get stored or processed.
5. Verify logging and alerting mechanisms detect and report validation failures.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-31, Node: actor-beacon-node, Supporting check for chain integrity

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does the gossip validation pipeline correctly verify that the finalized checkpoint is an ancestor of the sidecar parent block?
checklist.test_procedure:
1. Identify the gossip validation entry point for blob sidecars in the P2P layer.
2. Locate the specific validation logic that checks finalized checkpoint ancestry (q_check_finalized_ancestor).
3. Create a test sidecar with a parent block that is NOT a descendant of the current finalized checkpoint.
4. Submit the crafted sidecar through the gossip validation pipeline.
5. Verify that the sidecar is REJECTED with appropriate error code.
6. Confirm that no state changes or partial processing occurred for the invalid sidecar.
7. Test edge cases: sidecar from a fork that diverged just before finalization, sidecar with invalid parent block root.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-32, Trust Boundary: tb-001, Threat: stride-028

checklist.title: Can an attacker bypass finalized checkpoint ancestry verification by manipulating sidecar metadata?
checklist.test_procedure:
1. Review the sidecar structure to identify all fields used in ancestry verification.
2. Attempt to craft a sidecar with a valid-looking parent_root that references a block on a non-canonical chain.
3. Test if the validation correctly resolves the parent block and traces its ancestry to the finalized checkpoint.
4. Verify that the beacon node (actor-beacon-node) does not trust unverified parent references from P2P peers (actor-p2p-peer).
5. Check for race conditions where a sidecar could be processed before finality is updated.
6. Confirm that error handling does not leak information about internal chain state.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-32, Trust Boundary: tb-001, Covers: actor-p2p-peer -> actor-beacon-node

checklist.title: Does the beacon node maintain correct state when rejecting sidecars from non-canonical chains?
checklist.test_procedure:
1. Record the beacon node's state before processing an invalid sidecar.
2. Submit a sidecar from a non-canonical chain (one that does not descend from the finalized checkpoint).
3. Verify the sidecar is rejected at the gossip validation layer.
4. Confirm no partial state updates occurred (no orphaned data, no corrupted caches).
5. Verify the node's view of the canonical chain remains unchanged.
6. Test repeated invalid sidecar submissions to check for resource exhaustion or state drift.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-32, Supporting check for actor-beacon-node state integrity

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for tb-002: Does beacon block KZG validation correctly enforce epoch-dependent max_blobs_per_block limit?
checklist.test_procedure:
1. Identify the beacon block gossip validation code path that checks blob_kzg_commitments count.
2. Verify the validation retrieves max_blobs_per_block dynamically using get_blob_parameters(get_current_epoch(state)) rather than a hardcoded constant.
3. Test at epoch boundary: submit a block one slot before an epoch transition where max_blobs changes, with commitment count equal to the NEW epoch's limit (which exceeds the current epoch's limit).
4. Verify the block is rejected when commitment count exceeds the limit for the block's actual epoch.
5. Test edge case: submit block at exact epoch boundary slot and verify correct limit is applied.
6. Verify error messages clearly indicate the rejection reason without leaking internal state.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-33, Trust Boundary: tb-002, Primary Element: p2p:SG-007:beacon_block_kzg_validation, Source Invariant: p2p:SG-007:INV-001

checklist.title: P2P Peer Boundary Check: Can a malicious P2P peer bypass KZG commitment count validation through timing attacks at epoch boundaries?
checklist.test_procedure:
1. Identify all P2P message handlers that process beacon blocks with KZG commitments.
2. Analyze whether the epoch used for max_blobs calculation is derived from the block itself vs. current node state.
3. Test race condition: send block during epoch transition processing and verify consistent validation.
4. Verify that different clients (Prysm, Lighthouse, Teku, Nimbus, Lodestar) all compute max_blobs from the same source (block epoch vs state epoch).
5. Test with blocks claiming to be from future epochs and verify rejection.
6. Verify that attestation timing cannot be exploited to cause inconsistent max_blobs calculations.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-33, Node: actor-p2p-peer, Covers Trust Boundary: tb-002

checklist.title: Beacon Node Internal Validation: Is the epoch parameter correctly computed before being passed to get_blob_parameters()?
checklist.test_procedure:
1. Locate all call sites of get_blob_parameters() in block validation code.
2. Verify that get_current_epoch(state) is called with the correct state (pre-state of the block being validated, not current head state).
3. Trace the state parameter to ensure it cannot be substituted with a state from a different epoch.
4. Verify that epoch calculation handles slot-to-epoch conversion correctly at boundaries (slot % SLOTS_PER_EPOCH == 0).
5. Test with state at slot 0 of a new epoch and verify correct max_blobs is returned.
6. Check for any caching of max_blobs that could return stale values across epoch boundaries.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-33, Node: actor-beacon-node, Supporting check for boundary validation

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for tb-002: Does beacon block KZG validation correctly accept blocks with zero blob_kzg_commitments?
checklist.test_procedure:
1. Construct a valid beacon block with blob_kzg_commitments set to an empty list ([]).
2. Submit the block via P2P gossip to the target beacon node client.
3. Verify the block is NOT rejected during gossip validation (check for validation errors or dropped messages).
4. Monitor the beacon node logs for any error messages related to KZG commitment count validation.
5. Confirm the block propagates correctly through the network and is accepted into the beacon node's fork choice.
6. Repeat test across all supported consensus client implementations to verify consistent behavior.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-34, Trust Boundary: tb-002, Source Invariant: p2p:SG-007:INV-002. Rejecting valid empty-commitment blocks could cause consensus split.

checklist.title: Does the beacon node's block validation logic enforce a minimum commitment count that would incorrectly reject zero-commitment blocks?
checklist.test_procedure:
1. Review the source code of beacon_block_kzg_validation function for any lower bound checks on blob_kzg_commitments length.
2. Search for conditionals like 'len(commitments) < 1', 'len(commitments) == 0 => error', or similar minimum count enforcement.
3. Verify the validation logic only enforces upper bounds (MAX_BLOBS_PER_BLOCK) and not lower bounds.
4. Create a unit test that explicitly passes an empty commitment list and asserts validation succeeds.
5. Test edge case: block with commitments=[] but other blob-related fields present (should still be valid).
6. Verify the spec compliance by checking EIP-4844 / Deneb specifications for commitment count requirements.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-34, Node: actor-beacon-node, Primary Element: p2p:SG-007:beacon_block_kzg_validation. Incorrect lower bound check would be a consensus-critical bug.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for tb-003: Is the data_column_serve_range computation correct and are requests outside this range properly rejected with ResourceUnavailable(3)?
checklist.test_procedure:
1. Identify the DataColumnSidecarsByRange request handler in the P2P layer.
2. Verify the serve_range computation uses max(current_epoch - MIN_EPOCHS_FOR_DATA_COLUMN_SIDECARS_REQUESTS, FULU_FORK_EPOCH) as the lower bound.
3. Test boundary conditions: send requests for epochs at exactly (lower_bound - 1), exactly lower_bound, exactly current_epoch, and (current_epoch + 1).
4. Verify that requests at (lower_bound - 1) and (current_epoch + 1) return ResourceUnavailable(3) error code.
5. Check for off-by-one errors in the max() computation and range comparisons.
6. Verify the error response does not leak internal state or timing information.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-35, Trust Boundary: tb-003, Invariant: p2p:SG-008:INV-006. Attacker scenario: range requests targeting epochs outside serve range may cause stale data serving or incorrect rejection.

checklist.title: Does the beacon node correctly compute current_epoch and FULU_FORK_EPOCH for serve range calculation?
checklist.test_procedure:
1. Locate the code in actor-beacon-node that computes current_epoch for the serve range.
2. Verify current_epoch is derived from the current slot using correct epoch length constants.
3. Verify FULU_FORK_EPOCH is correctly configured and matches the network specification.
4. Test edge cases around fork transitions where FULU_FORK_EPOCH may affect the range.
5. Check for race conditions where current_epoch might change during request processing.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-35, Supporting Node: actor-beacon-node. Incorrect epoch computation could expand or contract the valid serve range.

checklist.title: Does the request-response client properly handle ResourceUnavailable(3) responses and prevent replay attacks with stale range requests?
checklist.test_procedure:
1. Identify how actor-req-resp-client handles ResourceUnavailable(3) responses from peers.
2. Verify the client does not retry the same out-of-range request indefinitely.
3. Check that the client correctly interprets the error code and adjusts future requests.
4. Test if an attacker can use ResourceUnavailable responses to cause denial of service.
5. Verify logging and metrics capture rejected requests for monitoring purposes.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-35, Supporting Node: actor-req-resp-client. Improper error handling could lead to resource exhaustion or state corruption.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for tb-003: Is the MAX_REQUEST_DATA_COLUMN_SIDECARS limit enforced as an absolute cap on DataColumnSidecarsByRange responses?
checklist.test_procedure:
1. Identify the DataColumnSidecarsByRange request handler in the P2P layer.
2. Locate where MAX_REQUEST_DATA_COLUMN_SIDECARS is defined and verify its value.
3. Craft a request with a large count value and multiple column indices that would exceed the limit.
4. Send the request and count the total number of sidecar chunks returned in the response.
5. Verify that the response contains no more than MAX_REQUEST_DATA_COLUMN_SIDECARS sidecars regardless of request parameters.
6. Test boundary conditions: request exactly at limit, one over limit, and significantly over limit.
7. Monitor server memory and bandwidth during large requests to confirm resource exhaustion is prevented.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-36, Trust Boundary: tb-003, Invariant: p2p:SG-008:INV-002, Threat: stride-009

checklist.title: Does the beacon node (actor-beacon-node) correctly enforce sidecar count limits before streaming responses to P2P clients?
checklist.test_procedure:
1. Review the beacon node's response generation logic for DataColumnSidecarsByRange.
2. Trace the code path from request parsing to response streaming.
3. Identify where the total sidecar count is calculated and where the limit check occurs.
4. Verify the limit is checked BEFORE sidecars are streamed (not after).
5. Confirm the count includes all sidecars across all requested columns and slots.
6. Test that partial responses are correctly terminated when the limit is reached mid-stream.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-36, Node: actor-beacon-node, Supporting check for boundary integrity

checklist.title: Does the request-response client (actor-req-resp-client) validate that received responses do not exceed expected sidecar limits?
checklist.test_procedure:
1. Review the client-side response handling for DataColumnSidecarsByRange.
2. Identify if there is a limit check on incoming sidecar chunks.
3. Simulate a malicious peer sending more than MAX_REQUEST_DATA_COLUMN_SIDECARS sidecars.
4. Verify the client terminates the connection or discards excess data.
5. Confirm that exceeding the limit does not cause memory exhaustion on the client side.
6. Check that appropriate error handling and peer scoring occurs when limits are violated.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-36, Node: actor-req-resp-client, Supporting check for boundary integrity

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for tb-003: Does DataColumnSidecarsByRange response enforce strict (slot, column_index) ordering?
checklist.test_procedure:
1. Set up a test environment with a beacon node responding to DataColumnSidecarsByRange requests over P2P.
2. Request a range of data column sidecars spanning multiple slots and column indices.
3. Verify that all received sidecars arrive in strict (slot, column_index) order: for consecutive sidecars S1 and S2, confirm (S1.slot < S2.slot) OR (S1.slot == S2.slot AND S1.column_index < S2.column_index).
4. Test edge cases: request ranges at slot boundaries, ranges with missing columns, and ranges at maximum values.
5. Inject or simulate a malicious server sending out-of-order responses and verify that receiving nodes either reject or handle the disorder gracefully.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-37, Trust Boundary: tb-003, Source Invariant: p2p:SG-008:INV-003, Source Assumption: asm-014. Out-of-order responses could cause syncing failures or incorrect data availability conclusions.

checklist.title: Does the beacon node's DataColumnSidecarsByRange handler guarantee post-condition ordering before transmission?
checklist.test_procedure:
1. Locate the beacon node implementation of the DataColumnSidecarsByRange response handler.
2. Trace the code path from data retrieval to response serialization and verify sorting logic exists.
3. Review the sorting implementation: confirm it uses (slot, column_index) as the sorting key with correct comparison semantics.
4. Check for race conditions or concurrent access patterns that could corrupt ordering.
5. Write or review unit tests that verify ordering is maintained across various input scenarios including empty responses, single-item responses, and responses at capacity limits.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-37, Node: actor-beacon-node. Server-side ordering guarantee must be enforced before data crosses trust boundary.

checklist.title: Does the requesting client validate or tolerate ordering violations in DataColumnSidecarsByRange responses?
checklist.test_procedure:
1. Locate the client-side implementation (actor-req-resp-client) that processes DataColumnSidecarsByRange responses.
2. Determine if the client relies on ordering for efficient processing (e.g., binary search, incremental state updates).
3. Simulate receiving an out-of-order response and observe client behavior: does it fail, process incorrectly, or handle gracefully?
4. Verify whether the client implements defensive validation to detect ordering violations.
5. Assess the impact of ordering violations on sync correctness and data availability conclusions.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-37, Node: actor-req-resp-client. Receiving nodes may rely on ordering for efficient processing; violations could cause syncing failures.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for tb-003: Does DataColumnSidecarsByRange abort response streaming when fork choice changes during an active request?
checklist.test_procedure:
1. Identify the DataColumnSidecarsByRange request handler in the P2P layer.
2. Locate the fork choice notification mechanism and how it interacts with active streaming responses.
3. Set up a test environment where a range request spans multiple blocks and takes measurable time to complete.
4. Trigger a fork choice update (reorg) while a DataColumnSidecarsByRange response is actively streaming.
5. Verify the server aborts the response rather than continuing to stream data from the pre-reorg chain.
6. Check that the client receives an appropriate error or stream termination signal.
7. Confirm no sidecars from the stale fork are included in the response after the fork choice change.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-38, Trust Boundary: tb-003, Source Invariant: p2p:SG-008:INV-005, Source Threat: stride-011

checklist.title: Does the beacon node correctly track fork choice state during DataColumnSidecarsByRange streaming and propagate changes to active handlers?
checklist.test_procedure:
1. Review the beacon node's fork choice tracking implementation and identify how fork choice updates are signaled.
2. Trace the code path from fork choice update to any active DataColumnSidecarsByRange handlers.
3. Verify there is a synchronization mechanism (lock, channel, or atomic flag) that allows streaming handlers to detect fork choice changes.
4. Test that fork choice updates during streaming are detected within an acceptable latency window.
5. Confirm that race conditions between fork choice updates and data retrieval are handled correctly.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-38, Supporting Node: actor-beacon-node, Source Invariant: p2p:SG-008:INV-005

checklist.title: Can an attacker exploit timing of DataColumnSidecarsByRange requests during reorgs to receive stale fork data?
checklist.test_procedure:
1. Analyze the request-response protocol from the perspective of a malicious P2P client (actor-req-resp-client).
2. Determine if an attacker can predict or induce reorgs to time their DataColumnSidecarsByRange requests.
3. Test sending multiple concurrent range requests and triggering reorgs to maximize the window for receiving stale data.
4. Verify that even partial responses from a stale fork are not useful for confusing syncing nodes.
5. Check if there are any client-side validation mechanisms that would detect and reject stale fork data.
6. Confirm the server implements consistent abort behavior regardless of request timing or attacker manipulation.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-38, Supporting Node: actor-req-resp-client, Attacker scenario from reachability notes

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for tb-004: Does DataColumnSidecarsByRoot compute total requested_columns_count across ALL identifiers before validating against MAX_REQUEST_DATA_COLUMN_SIDECARS?
checklist.test_procedure:
1. Locate the DataColumnSidecarsByRoot request handler in the P2P layer.
2. Identify where requested_columns_count is computed and verify it sums len(r.columns) for ALL identifiers in the request.
3. Craft a malicious request with N identifiers, each requesting M columns where N*M > MAX_REQUEST_DATA_COLUMN_SIDECARS but each individual M is small (e.g., 1000 identifiers with 1 column each).
4. Send the crafted request via the P2P protocol and observe whether the request is rejected.
5. Verify the validation occurs BEFORE any resource-intensive processing begins.
6. Check error handling ensures the ENTIRE request is rejected, not just individual identifiers.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-39, Trust Boundary: tb-004, Invariant: p2p:SG-009:INV-001, Threat: stride-012

checklist.title: Does the beacon node (actor-beacon-node) properly reject P2P requests where aggregated column count exceeds MAX_REQUEST_DATA_COLUMN_SIDECARS?
checklist.test_procedure:
1. Review the beacon node's request handling code for DataColumnSidecarsByRoot.
2. Trace the code path from P2P message receipt to the validation logic.
3. Verify that the aggregation logic uses sum(len(r.columns) for r in request) and not per-identifier checks.
4. Test with boundary values: exactly at MAX_REQUEST_DATA_COLUMN_SIDECARS, one above, and one below.
5. Confirm that resource allocation (memory, CPU) does not occur before validation completes.
6. Verify that rejection happens atomically for the entire request batch.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-39, Node: actor-beacon-node, Trust Boundary: tb-004

checklist.title: Can a malicious req-resp client (actor-req-resp-client) bypass the column count limit by distributing requests across multiple identifiers?
checklist.test_procedure:
1. Create a test harness that can send arbitrary DataColumnSidecarsByRoot requests.
2. Implement the specific attack pattern: 1000 identifiers each requesting 1 column.
3. Send the request and monitor the target node's resource consumption (memory, CPU, I/O).
4. Verify the request is rejected with an appropriate error code before processing begins.
5. Repeat with varying distributions (e.g., 100 identifiers with 10 columns each, 500 with 2 each).
6. Confirm that no partial processing occurs - rejection must be all-or-nothing.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-39, Node: actor-req-resp-client, PoC: send request with 1000 identifiers each requesting 1 column (total=1000) exceeding limit.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for tb-004: Is the minimum_request_epoch correctly computed and enforced for DataColumnSidecarsByRoot requests?
checklist.test_procedure:
1. Locate the DataColumnSidecarsByRoot request handler in the P2P layer.
2. Identify where minimum_request_epoch is computed and verify it uses max(current_epoch - MIN_EPOCHS_FOR_DATA_COLUMN_SIDECARS_REQUESTS, FULU_FORK_EPOCH).
3. Craft a P2P request for data column sidecars with an epoch value below the minimum_request_epoch.
4. Send the malicious request and observe the server response - it must return ResourceUnavailable or skip the request without database scanning.
5. Monitor I/O activity during the request to confirm no database amplification occurs.
6. Test edge cases: epoch exactly at minimum, epoch one below minimum, epoch at FULU_FORK_EPOCH boundary.
7. Verify the error response does not leak internal state information.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-40, Trust Boundary: tb-004, Source Invariant: p2p:SG-009:INV-002

checklist.title: Does the Beacon Node correctly reject DataColumnSidecarsByRoot requests for epochs before FULU_FORK_EPOCH?
checklist.test_procedure:
1. Identify the beacon node component that processes DataColumnSidecarsByRoot requests.
2. Review the code path from P2P request reception to database query.
3. Submit a request with epoch = FULU_FORK_EPOCH - 1 and verify it is rejected before any database access.
4. Submit a request with epoch = 0 (genesis) and verify immediate rejection.
5. Use tracing or logging to confirm no database queries are executed for invalid epoch requests.
6. Measure response latency for invalid requests - should be minimal (no I/O wait).
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-40, Node: actor-beacon-node, Source Invariant: p2p:SG-009:INV-002

checklist.title: Is the request-response client properly validating epoch bounds before forwarding DataColumnSidecarsByRoot requests?
checklist.test_procedure:
1. Locate the request-response client handler for DataColumnSidecarsByRoot.
2. Verify that epoch validation occurs at the earliest possible point in request processing.
3. Confirm the client computes minimum_request_epoch using the correct formula: max(current_epoch - MIN_EPOCHS_FOR_DATA_COLUMN_SIDECARS_REQUESTS, FULU_FORK_EPOCH).
4. Test that requests with stale epochs are rejected at the client layer without propagating to backend services.
5. Verify rate limiting or connection penalties are applied to peers sending repeated invalid epoch requests.
6. Check that the validation logic is not bypassable through request field manipulation.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-40, Node: actor-req-resp-client, Source Invariant: p2p:SG-009:INV-002

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for tb-004: Does DataColumnSidecarsByRoot reject requests for sidecars of blocks that failed gossip validation?
checklist.test_procedure:
1. Identify the DataColumnSidecarsByRoot RPC handler in the codebase.
2. Trace the code path to determine how it validates block roots before serving sidecars.
3. Verify that gossip validation status is checked before serving any sidecar data.
4. Attempt to request sidecars for a block root that exists in the database but failed gossip validation.
5. Confirm that the server returns an appropriate error or empty response rather than serving the sidecar.
6. Check if timing or error message differences could leak information about block existence in the database.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-41, Trust Boundary: tb-004, Invariant: p2p:SG-009:INV-004, Threat: stride-013

checklist.title: Does the beacon node maintain accurate gossip validation state to prevent serving invalid sidecars?
checklist.test_procedure:
1. Review how the beacon node tracks gossip validation status for blocks.
2. Verify that blocks failing gossip validation are properly marked in the database or state.
3. Check for race conditions where a block's validation status could change between check and serve.
4. Test scenarios where a block initially passes validation but is later invalidated.
5. Verify that state transition failures are also recorded and checked before serving sidecars.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-41, Node: actor-beacon-node, Supports boundary check CHK-W4B13-41-001

checklist.title: Can a malicious request-response client exploit timing or response patterns to determine block existence in the database?
checklist.test_procedure:
1. Analyze the response handling for DataColumnSidecarsByRoot requests.
2. Measure response times for: (a) non-existent blocks, (b) existing invalid blocks, (c) existing valid blocks.
3. Compare error responses and codes between the three cases to identify distinguishing patterns.
4. Verify that the server does not leak block existence through side channels.
5. Test rate limiting and access controls on the RPC endpoint to prevent oracle attacks.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-41, Node: actor-req-resp-client, Supports boundary check CHK-W4B13-41-001

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for tb-007/tb-008: Is blob data from EL via engine_getBlobsV2 validated before conversion to data columns?
checklist.test_procedure:
1. Identify the code path where engine_getBlobsV2 response is processed in the beacon node.
2. Trace the blob data flow from EL response to data column construction.
3. Verify whether any KZG commitment verification occurs on blobs received from EL.
4. Simulate a scenario where EL returns malformed or corrupted blob data and observe CL behavior.
5. Check if corrupted data columns would be gossipped to the P2P network without rejection.
6. Verify JWT authentication is enforced on the EngineAPI boundary.
7. Document the trust boundary assumptions and assess if they are appropriate for the threat model.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-42, Trust Boundaries: tb-007, tb-008, Invariant: p2p:SG-010:INV-001. Documented trust assumption that EL is JWT-authenticated and co-located. Missing KZG verification represents significant attack surface if EL is compromised.

checklist.title: Does the beacon node implement defense-in-depth for blob data integrity despite JWT-authenticated EL trust assumption?
checklist.test_procedure:
1. Review the distributed_blob_publishing implementation to understand the data flow from EL to gossip publication.
2. Identify all validation checks (if any) performed on blob data before column construction.
3. Assess whether the lack of KZG verification could be exploited if JWT credentials are compromised.
4. Verify that receiving nodes perform KZG verification on data columns before accepting them.
5. Evaluate the impact of publishing invalid data columns on the node's reputation score.
6. Document recommendations for adding optional KZG verification as defense-in-depth.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-42, Supporting check for actor-beacon-node. The trust assumption that EL is safe due to JWT authentication may not hold under all threat scenarios.

checklist.title: Can corrupted data columns from a compromised EL propagate to gossip subscribers and affect network-wide data availability?
checklist.test_procedure:
1. Map the P2P gossip topology for data column distribution (actor-gossip-subscriber interactions).
2. Trace how data columns flow from the publishing beacon node to subscribing peers.
3. Verify that gossip subscribers validate data columns via KZG before forwarding or storing.
4. Test whether invalid data columns are rejected at the P2P layer with appropriate error handling.
5. Assess the blast radius if validation is missing or insufficient at any hop.
6. Document the potential for denial-of-service through reputation damage to the publishing node.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-42, Supporting check for actor-gossip-subscriber. Invalid data columns could damage publisher reputation and delay data availability across the network.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for tb-008: Does distributed blob publishing enforce subnet subscription checks before publishing data columns?
checklist.test_procedure:
1. Identify all code paths where data columns are published to the gossip network.
2. Review the subscription state management for custody and sampling subnets.
3. Verify that before any publish operation, the code checks if the target subnet is in the node's current subscription list.
4. Attempt to trigger a publish operation for a subnet the node is not subscribed to and verify it is blocked.
5. Test race conditions where subscription state changes during publish operations.
6. Verify error handling and logging when a publish to non-subscribed subnet is attempted.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-43, Trust Boundary: tb-008, Source Invariant: p2p:SG-010:INV-002

checklist.title: Does the beacon node correctly maintain and enforce the subscription state for custody and sampling subnets during blob publishing?
checklist.test_procedure:
1. Locate the beacon node's subscription management logic for custody and sampling subnets.
2. Trace how subscription state is initialized, updated, and queried during blob publishing.
3. Verify that subscription state is consistent across all components that perform publishing.
4. Test scenarios where subnet subscriptions are added or removed while publish operations are in progress.
5. Check for any state synchronization issues between the gossip subscriber and the publishing component.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-43, Node: actor-beacon-node, Source Invariant: p2p:SG-010:INV-002

checklist.title: Does the gossip subscriber properly communicate subscription state to prevent publishing on non-subscribed subnets?
checklist.test_procedure:
1. Review how the gossip subscriber tracks and exposes its current subnet subscriptions.
2. Identify the interface between the gossip subscriber and the blob publishing logic.
3. Verify that subscription state changes are propagated atomically and consistently.
4. Test edge cases where the gossip subscriber's internal state differs from what is exposed to publishing logic.
5. Check for any caching or stale state issues that could lead to publishing on unsubscribed subnets.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-43, Node: actor-gossip-subscriber, Source Invariant: p2p:SG-010:INV-002

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is the anti-equivocation cache unconditionally updated when data columns are imported via P2P?
checklist.test_procedure:
1. Identify all code paths where data columns are imported into the beacon node (both via gossip and local block production).
2. Trace each import path to verify the anti-equivocation cache update is called unconditionally.
3. Check for conditional logic that could skip cache updates (e.g., publication success checks, error handling branches).
4. Create a test scenario where a data column import succeeds but gossip publication fails, and verify the cache is still updated.
5. Attempt to import conflicting data columns from the same proposer after a failed publication to verify equivocation detection still works.
6. Review error handling paths to ensure cache updates are not rolled back on subsequent failures.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-44, Trust Boundary: tb-008, Invariant: p2p:SG-010:INV-003

checklist.title: Can an equivocating proposer bypass anti-equivocation detection by exploiting conditional cache update logic in distributed blob publishing?
checklist.test_procedure:
1. Set up a test network with multiple beacon nodes subscribed to different data column subnets.
2. Craft two conflicting data column sets from the same proposer for the same slot.
3. Simulate a scenario where the first set is imported locally but publication to gossip fails or is delayed.
4. Publish the second (conflicting) set to a different subnet that the local node monitors.
5. Verify whether the local node detects the equivocation by checking if both sets are flagged as conflicting.
6. If equivocation is not detected, identify the code path that failed to update the anti-equivocation cache.
7. Document the race condition or conditional logic that enabled the bypass.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-44, Threat: stride-016, Assumption: asm-007

checklist.title: Does the beacon node correctly update anti-equivocation state for all data column import scenarios including local block production?
checklist.test_procedure:
1. Enumerate all code paths in the beacon node where data columns can be imported (gossip reception, local block production, sync, RPC).
2. For each path, verify the anti-equivocation cache update is invoked before any conditional checks.
3. Test the local block production path specifically: produce a block with data columns and verify cache update occurs.
4. Verify that cache updates happen atomically with data column storage to prevent TOCTOU issues.
5. Check that the cache key generation is consistent across all import paths to ensure proper equivocation detection.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-44, Node: actor-beacon-node

checklist.title: Is there a post-condition violation where anti-equivocation cache state diverges from actual imported data column state?
checklist.test_procedure:
1. Define the post-condition invariant: After any successful data column import, the anti-equivocation cache MUST contain an entry for that (proposer, slot, column_index) tuple.
2. Instrument the code to log cache state before and after each import operation.
3. Run a comprehensive test suite covering all import paths and verify the invariant holds.
4. Specifically test failure scenarios: partial imports, rollbacks, and error recovery paths.
5. Verify that the cache entry content matches the imported data (commitment hash) to prevent cache poisoning.
6. Test concurrent imports of the same data column to verify no race conditions in cache updates.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-44, Type: post-condition, Primary Element: p2p:SG-010:distributed_blob_publishing

checklist.title: Can data columns imported via distributed blob publishing bypass data availability checks or fork choice integration that would be applied to gossip-received columns?
checklist.test_procedure:
1. Identify the code paths for importing data columns via distributed blob publishing (e.g., EngineAPI or local import mechanisms).
2. Identify the code paths for receiving data columns via gossip (P2P layer).
3. Trace both paths to their integration points with data availability checks and fork choice processing.
4. Compare the validation steps, state updates, and downstream processing applied to columns from each source.
5. Attempt to import a data column via the distributed blob publishing path and verify it is counted for data availability in the same manner as gossip-received columns.
6. Test edge case: Import a column, then verify that a block depending on that column's availability is correctly accepted by fork choice.
7. Verify that no code paths allow imported columns to be treated as 'already validated' without performing the same checks as gossip columns.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-45, Invariant: p2p:SG-010:INV-004, Primary Element: p2p:SG-010:distributed_blob_publishing, Trust Boundary: tb-008. Bug bounty note: Demonstrate that a locally-imported data column is not counted for data availability, causing the node to reject a valid block.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for tb-007/tb-008: Does distributed blob publishing abort when engine_getBlobsV2 returns incomplete blobs?
checklist.test_procedure:
1. Identify the distributed blob publishing code path that calls engine_getBlobsV2.
2. Review how the response from engine_getBlobsV2 is validated against block kzg_commitments.
3. Simulate a scenario where EL returns fewer blobs than expected (e.g., 3 of 6 blobs).
4. Verify that the publishing function aborts entirely without publishing any data columns.
5. Check for race conditions where partial data might be published before validation completes.
6. Confirm that no partial state is persisted when abort occurs.
7. Verify error logging captures the mismatch for debugging purposes.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-46, Trust Boundaries: tb-007, tb-008, Assumption: asm-006, Threat: stride-015

checklist.title: Does the Beacon Node correctly validate blob count from Execution Layer before initiating data column publication?
checklist.test_procedure:
1. Trace the data flow from engine_getBlobsV2 response handling in the Beacon Node.
2. Identify where blob count validation against kzg_commitments occurs.
3. Verify that the comparison is exact (count must match, not just be non-zero).
4. Test with edge cases: zero blobs returned, one blob missing, all blobs missing.
5. Confirm that validation happens before any publication logic is triggered.
6. Check that the validation logic cannot be bypassed through error handling paths.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-46, Node: actor-beacon-node, Assumption: asm-006

checklist.title: Is the atomicity of blob publishing guaranteed such that partial publication cannot occur during EL communication failures?
checklist.test_procedure:
1. Review the distributed blob publishing implementation for atomicity guarantees.
2. Identify all code paths that could lead to partial data column publication.
3. Test network timeout scenarios during engine_getBlobsV2 calls.
4. Verify rollback mechanisms exist if publication is interrupted mid-process.
5. Check for TOCTOU vulnerabilities between blob retrieval and publication.
6. Simulate concurrent requests that might interfere with atomicity.
7. Confirm that data availability guarantees are maintained under failure conditions.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-46, Element: p2p:SG-010:distributed_blob_publishing, Threat: stride-015

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for tb-005: Is the BlobSidecarsByRange deprecated protocol fully disabled after FULU_FORK_EPOCH + MIN_EPOCHS_FOR_BLOB_SIDECARS_REQUESTS?
checklist.test_procedure:
1. Identify the code path handling BlobSidecarsByRange requests in the P2P layer.
2. Verify that an epoch check exists comparing current_epoch against FULU_FORK_EPOCH + MIN_EPOCHS_FOR_BLOB_SIDECARS_REQUESTS.
3. Simulate a request after the deprecation threshold and confirm the server returns empty or rejects the request.
4. Attempt to send malformed BlobSidecarsByRange requests post-deprecation to verify no legacy code paths are triggered.
5. Review error handling to ensure no resource exhaustion or information leakage occurs when deprecated requests are received.
6. Confirm that the protocol handler is completely unreachable (not just returning errors) after the deprecation epoch.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-47, Trust Boundary: tb-005, Source Invariant: p2p:SG-011:INV-001, Threat: stride-023

checklist.title: Does the beacon node properly reject deprecated BlobSidecarsByRange requests from external P2P clients after the transition period?
checklist.test_procedure:
1. Set up a test environment where current_epoch exceeds FULU_FORK_EPOCH + MIN_EPOCHS_FOR_BLOB_SIDECARS_REQUESTS.
2. From an external P2P client (actor-req-resp-client), send a BlobSidecarsByRange request to the beacon node.
3. Verify the beacon node (actor-beacon-node) either returns an empty response or explicitly rejects the request.
4. Monitor resource consumption on the beacon node to ensure no significant processing occurs for deprecated requests.
5. Test boundary conditions: send requests exactly at the deprecation epoch and one epoch before/after to verify correct threshold enforcement.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-47, Covered Nodes: actor-req-resp-client, actor-beacon-node, Trust Boundary: tb-005

checklist.title: Are legacy code paths for BlobSidecarsByRange properly isolated and unreachable after protocol deprecation?
checklist.test_procedure:
1. Perform static analysis to identify all code paths that handle BlobSidecarsByRange requests.
2. Verify that epoch validation occurs before any processing logic is invoked.
3. Confirm that no race conditions exist where requests received at the boundary epoch could still be processed.
4. Attempt to trigger legacy code paths through crafted requests that might bypass epoch checks (e.g., manipulated timestamps, replay attacks).
5. Verify that deprecated protocol handlers do not expose implementation bugs or vulnerabilities even if somehow reached.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-47, Primary Element: p2p:SG-011:blob_sidecars_by_range_deprecated, Assumption: asm-010

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for tb-005: Is BlobSidecarsByRange request properly validated to enforce serve_range boundaries during the Fulu transition period?
checklist.test_procedure:
1. Identify the BlobSidecarsByRange request handler in the P2P layer.
2. Construct a request with start_slot before FULU_FORK_EPOCH and count extending past FULU_FORK_EPOCH.
3. Verify the response only includes blob sidecars from the valid serve_range [max(current_epoch - MIN_EPOCHS_FOR_BLOB_SIDECARS_REQUESTS, DENEB_FORK_EPOCH), FULU_FORK_EPOCH).
4. Send a request entirely at or after FULU_FORK_EPOCH and verify an empty list is returned.
5. Test edge cases: request starting exactly at FULU_FORK_EPOCH-1, request ending exactly at FULU_FORK_EPOCH.
6. Verify no crash or panic occurs when processing boundary-spanning requests.
7. Check that post-Fulu data is never served via the deprecated BlobSidecarsByRange protocol.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-48, Trust Boundary: tb-005, Invariant: p2p:SG-011:INV-002. PoC: request blob sidecars with range spanning FULU_FORK_EPOCH and observe crash or incorrect response.

checklist.title: Does the Request-Response Client correctly calculate and enforce the serve_range boundaries for deprecated BlobSidecarsByRange?
checklist.test_procedure:
1. Locate the serve_range calculation logic in the request-response client code.
2. Verify the formula: serve_range = [max(current_epoch - MIN_EPOCHS_FOR_BLOB_SIDECARS_REQUESTS, DENEB_FORK_EPOCH), FULU_FORK_EPOCH).
3. Test with various current_epoch values to ensure correct boundary calculation.
4. Verify that MIN_EPOCHS_FOR_BLOB_SIDECARS_REQUESTS is correctly applied.
5. Check that DENEB_FORK_EPOCH is used as the lower bound when current_epoch is close to genesis.
6. Confirm FULU_FORK_EPOCH is used as the exclusive upper bound.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-48, Node: actor-req-resp-client, Supporting check for boundary integrity.

checklist.title: Does the Beacon Node correctly filter blob sidecars to only serve pre-Fulu data via the deprecated BlobSidecarsByRange endpoint?
checklist.test_procedure:
1. Identify the beacon node's blob sidecar storage and retrieval logic.
2. Verify the filtering logic that prevents post-Fulu blob sidecars from being served.
3. Test that blob sidecars at exactly FULU_FORK_EPOCH are NOT served.
4. Test that blob sidecars at FULU_FORK_EPOCH - 1 ARE served (if within serve_range).
5. Verify that the beacon node returns an empty response (not an error) for out-of-range requests.
6. Check for any race conditions during the fork transition that could leak post-Fulu data.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-48, Node: actor-beacon-node, Supporting check for data filtering.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for tb-005/tb-006: Are peers protected from scoring penalties when using deprecated BlobSidecarsByRange protocol during FULU fork transition?
checklist.test_procedure:
1. Identify the peer scoring logic for the BlobSidecarsByRange request handler in the P2P layer.
2. Locate the deprecated protocol handler for blob_sidecars_by_range and verify it exists for the transition period.
3. Simulate a legitimate peer sending a BlobSidecarsByRange request during FULU_FORK_EPOCH transition window.
4. Monitor peer score changes after the deprecated protocol request is processed.
5. Verify that peer score remains unchanged (no penalty applied) for using the deprecated protocol.
6. Test edge cases: requests at exact epoch boundary, requests slightly before and after the deprecation window.
7. Confirm that appropriate error responses are returned without scoring penalties.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-49, Trust Boundaries: tb-005, tb-006, Invariant: p2p:SG-011:INV-003. Network fragmentation risk if older nodes are penalized during transition.

checklist.title: Does the Request-Response Client (actor-req-resp-client) gracefully handle deprecated BlobSidecarsByRange responses without triggering peer penalties?
checklist.test_procedure:
1. Locate the request-response client code that handles BlobSidecarsByRange protocol responses.
2. Identify all code paths where peer scoring adjustments are made in the client.
3. Trace the handling of deprecated protocol responses (e.g., RESOURCE_UNAVAILABLE, unsupported protocol errors).
4. Verify that receiving a deprecation-related error does not decrement the peer's score.
5. Test with various deprecation error responses to ensure consistent non-penalty behavior.
6. Confirm graceful degradation: client should fall back to alternative data retrieval methods without penalizing the peer.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-49, Supporting Node: actor-req-resp-client. Client-side scoring decisions could fragment the network if deprecated protocol errors are treated as misbehavior.

checklist.title: Does the Beacon Node (actor-beacon-node) correctly distinguish between deprecated protocol usage and malicious peer behavior when adjusting scores?
checklist.test_procedure:
1. Locate the beacon node's peer scoring logic for P2P request handling.
2. Identify how the beacon node differentiates between deprecated protocol requests and invalid/malicious requests.
3. Verify that score penalty logic includes explicit checks for FULU_FORK_EPOCH transition period.
4. Test that deprecated BlobSidecarsByRange requests from peers are not classified as protocol violations.
5. Confirm that the scoring system has a whitelist or exception for deprecated protocols during transition.
6. Verify logging: deprecated protocol usage should be logged at INFO/DEBUG level, not WARNING/ERROR.
7. Test that legitimate deprecation does not trigger rate limiting or connection termination.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-49, Supporting Node: actor-beacon-node. Beacon node is responsible for maintaining network connectivity during protocol transitions.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is the ENR eth2 field validated to be exactly 16 bytes before SSZ deserialization?
checklist.test_procedure:
1. Identify the ENR parsing code path that handles the eth2 field in the P2P discovery layer.
2. Locate the SSZ deserialization function for ENRForkID and verify it validates input length before parsing.
3. Craft an ENR record with an eth2 field shorter than 16 bytes (e.g., 8 bytes) and submit via P2P discovery.
4. Craft an ENR record with an eth2 field longer than 16 bytes (e.g., 32 bytes) and submit via P2P discovery.
5. Verify both malformed ENR records are rejected with appropriate error handling (no crash, no buffer over-read).
6. Check that error messages do not leak internal memory contents or sensitive information.
7. Verify the peer is not added to the peer table when ENR validation fails.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-50, Trust Boundary: tb-009, Invariant: p2p:SG-012:INV-001

checklist.title: Can a malicious P2P peer cause buffer over-read or under-read by sending ENR eth2 fields with invalid lengths?
checklist.test_procedure:
1. Review the SSZ deserialization code for ENRForkID to identify how it handles length validation.
2. Trace the data flow from raw P2P message receipt to ENRForkID struct population.
3. Test with eth2 field containing 0 bytes (empty) to verify rejection without crash.
4. Test with eth2 field containing 15 bytes (one byte short) to verify rejection.
5. Test with eth2 field containing 17 bytes (one byte extra) to verify rejection.
6. Use memory sanitizers (ASan/MSan) during testing to detect any buffer over-read or under-read.
7. Verify that rejected ENR records do not cause the node to disconnect from the P2P network.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-50, Trust Boundary: tb-009, Threat: stride-017

checklist.title: Does the beacon node properly reject peers with invalid ENR eth2 fields during discovery handshake?
checklist.test_procedure:
1. Identify the peer discovery handshake flow between actor-p2p-peer and actor-beacon-node.
2. Locate where ENR records are exchanged and validated during peer discovery.
3. Set up a test P2P peer that advertises an ENR with a malformed eth2 field.
4. Attempt to connect to the beacon node and observe if the connection is rejected.
5. Verify that the beacon node logs an appropriate warning/error for the invalid ENR.
6. Confirm the malicious peer is not added to the peer routing table.
7. Test that repeated invalid ENR attempts trigger rate limiting or peer banning.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-50, Covered nodes: actor-p2p-peer, actor-beacon-node

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for tb-009: Does BPO fork ENR eth2 field computation correctly preserve next_fork_version equal to current_fork_version?
checklist.test_procedure:
1. Identify the ENR eth2 field computation code path for BPO (Blob Parameter Only) forks.
2. Verify that for BPO forks, next_fork_version is set equal to current_fork_version (not a new version).
3. Confirm that next_fork_epoch is set to the planned BPO fork epoch.
4. Compare the implementation against regular fork handling where next_fork_version != current_fork_version.
5. Test peer discovery between two nodes at a BPO fork boundary to ensure they correctly discover each other.
6. Attempt to trigger network fragmentation by manipulating ENR eth2 field values at BPO fork boundaries.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-51, Trust Boundary: tb-009, Source Invariant: p2p:SG-012:INV-002, Source Threat: stride-018

checklist.title: Does the P2P peer actor correctly validate incoming ENR eth2 fields for BPO fork compatibility?
checklist.test_procedure:
1. Locate the ENR validation logic in the P2P peer handling code.
2. Verify that BPO fork detection correctly identifies when a fork is BPO vs. regular.
3. Test that peers with correctly computed BPO fork ENR eth2 fields are accepted.
4. Test that peers with incorrectly computed ENR eth2 fields (next_fork_version != current_fork_version for BPO) are handled appropriately.
5. Verify the validation logic does not inadvertently reject compatible peers at BPO fork boundaries.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-51, Supporting Node: actor-p2p-peer, Source Invariant: p2p:SG-012:INV-002

checklist.title: Does the beacon node correctly compute and advertise ENR eth2 field for BPO forks?
checklist.test_procedure:
1. Locate the beacon node's ENR eth2 field computation logic.
2. Identify how the beacon node determines whether an upcoming fork is BPO or regular.
3. Verify the beacon node sets next_fork_version = current_fork_version for BPO forks.
4. Verify the beacon node sets next_fork_epoch to the planned BPO epoch.
5. Test beacon node ENR advertisement at various epochs relative to a BPO fork activation.
6. Compare ENR output between different client implementations to ensure consistency.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-51, Supporting Node: actor-beacon-node, Source Invariant: p2p:SG-012:INV-002

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for tb-009: Is the ENR eth2 field correctly signaling no upcoming fork by setting next_fork_version == current_fork_version AND next_fork_epoch == FAR_FUTURE_EPOCH?
checklist.test_procedure:
1. Identify the ENR eth2 field computation logic in the beacon node codebase (p2p:SG-012:enr_eth2_field_computation).
2. Review the code path that determines when no fork is planned and how the ENR fields are set.
3. Verify that when no fork is scheduled, next_fork_version is set equal to current_fork_version.
4. Verify that when no fork is scheduled, next_fork_epoch is set to FAR_FUTURE_EPOCH (0xFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFF or equivalent).
5. Test by simulating a state with no planned fork and inspecting the advertised ENR eth2 field values.
6. Verify that peers correctly interpret the no-fork signal and do not reject the node as being on a different fork schedule.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-52, Trust Boundary: tb-009, Invariant: p2p:SG-012:INV-003, Threat: stride-017

checklist.title: Does the beacon node (actor-beacon-node) correctly compute ENR eth2 fields for peer discovery when transitioning between fork states?
checklist.test_procedure:
1. Locate the beacon node's ENR update logic that handles fork schedule changes.
2. Review state transitions: verify the node correctly updates ENR fields when moving from 'fork planned' to 'no fork planned' state.
3. Test edge case: immediately after a fork completes, verify the node resets next_fork_version and next_fork_epoch to the no-fork values.
4. Verify that ENR updates are propagated to peers in a timely manner after fork state changes.
5. Check for race conditions where ENR might temporarily advertise stale fork information.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-52, Supporting Node: actor-beacon-node, Invariant: p2p:SG-012:INV-003

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for tb-009: Does the ENR eth2 field computation use wall-clock time instead of sync state for epoch determination?
checklist.test_procedure:
1. Identify the code responsible for computing the ENR eth2 field fork_digest in the beacon node.
2. Trace the epoch value used in fork_digest calculation to determine its source (wall-clock vs sync state).
3. Set up a test node that is still syncing (behind chain head by multiple epochs).
4. Capture the ENR advertisement from the syncing node and extract the fork_digest field.
5. Compare the advertised fork_digest against what would be expected from: (a) wall-clock epoch, (b) sync-state epoch.
6. Verify that the fork_digest matches the wall-clock epoch, not the synced-to epoch.
7. Test across a simulated fork boundary where a syncing node would be behind the fork but wall-clock time is past the fork.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-53, Trust Boundary: tb-009, Invariant: p2p:SG-012:INV-004. A syncing node using sync-state epoch would advertise a pre-fork fork_digest after the fork has occurred, causing it to be filtered out by fully-synced peers.

checklist.title: Can a syncing beacon node become isolated from the P2P network by advertising an incorrect pre-fork fork_digest?
checklist.test_procedure:
1. Deploy a test beacon node and allow it to fully sync to chain head.
2. Configure a second test node to start syncing from genesis (or significantly behind chain head).
3. Simulate a network fork transition while the second node is still syncing.
4. Monitor peer discovery attempts from the syncing node to fully-synced peers.
5. Verify whether the syncing node can successfully connect to post-fork peers.
6. Check ENR advertisements from the syncing node for fork_digest correctness.
7. Document any peer filtering or connection rejections that occur due to fork_digest mismatch.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-53, Node: actor-beacon-node. Bug bounty target: Demonstrate a syncing node being isolated because it advertises pre-fork fork_digest.

checklist.title: Does the beacon node correctly update its ENR eth2 field when wall-clock time crosses a fork epoch boundary?
checklist.test_procedure:
1. Identify the ENR update mechanism and the trigger for eth2 field updates.
2. Set up a beacon node with a known fork schedule where a fork is imminent.
3. Monitor the node's ENR eth2 field continuously as wall-clock time approaches the fork epoch.
4. Verify that the ENR is updated precisely when wall-clock time crosses the fork boundary.
5. Confirm that the update occurs regardless of the node's sync state (even if still syncing pre-fork blocks).
6. Test edge cases: node restart near fork boundary, network partition during fork transition.
7. Validate that the sequence number in ENR is incremented appropriately on fork_digest change.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-53, Trust Boundary: tb-009. Fork transitions must be handled based on wall-clock time to prevent bootstrapping problems.
